energy.headline: Battery Storage Surging as U.S. Power Demand Climbs and Households Battle Rising Electricity Bills
energy.summary: Home battery capacity increased by 51% in 2025, with homeowners seeking relief from high energy costs and protection against extreme weather. Analysts expect the residential market to grow another 120% by 2030.

energy.headline: New Hampshire Net Metering 2.0 Guide for 2026
energy.summary: New Hampshire maintains Net Metering 2.0 as its foundational policy for residential solar, with adoption growing 12% annually. The framework balances benefits of distributed generation with grid maintenance costs.

energy.headline: Solar cells achieve 130% energy conversion efficiency breakthrough
energy.summary: Researchers developed a spin-flip metal complex capturing and multiplying energy from sunlight through singlet fission, exceeding the traditional 100% efficiency limit. This breakthrough points toward more advanced solar technologies.

energy.headline: WoodMac: US Energy Storage Deployment Increased by 52% in 2025
energy.summary: The U.S. energy storage market hit a record 18.9 GW of BESS installations in 2025. Residential storage hit a quarterly milestone in Q4 with 1 GWh installed as tax incentive expiration accelerated demand.

energy.headline: Plug-in solar panels gaining momentum but utilities push back on safety concerns
energy.summary: Easy-to-install plug-in solar panels are getting attention as Americans worry about rising energy costs, with 30 similar bills proposed across the U.S. Utilities are raising safety concerns and successfully delaying votes in 5 states.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves and energy prices soar
energy.summary: As net metering rules change and electricity prices climb, homeowners are increasingly storing excess solar power for themselves rather than exporting to the grid. Battery adoption is now central to home solar strategy.

energy.headline: Colorado clearing way for portable, plug-in solar at home
energy.summary: Colorado is joining dozens of states allowing portable plug-in solar panels, with proposed meter collars enabling affordable $500-700 installations instead of $10,000 panel upgrades. Systems cost ~$2,000 with storage batteries.

energy.headline: Solar residential market experiences historic 205% surge before tax credit expiration
energy.summary: Homeowners rushed to install solar before the 30% federal tax credit ended December 31, 2025, creating one of the largest demand surges the industry ever experienced. Most installers reached annual capacity by October 2025.

energy.headline: Most U.S. households can save money and weather blackouts with solar plus storage
energy.summary: About 60% of families could reduce electricity costs by 15% by installing solar-battery systems, though benefits don't align equally with areas that need them most. Policy changes are reducing incentives for new installations.

energy.headline: Solar and storage to lead record-breaking 86 GW of new U.S. capacity in 2026
energy.summary: Solar and battery storage account for 79% of 86 GW of new utility-scale capacity planned in 2026, marking the largest annual increase in over two decades. Renewables projected to account for 93% of all new capacity.

energy.headline: California net metering court defeat for rooftop solar owners
energy.summary: An appeals court upheld 2022 cuts that reduced utility payments for excess solar power by about 75%, siding with regulators who argue solar owners don't pay their fair share of grid maintenance costs.

energy.headline: Net Metering Policies by State: Complete US Guide 2026
energy.summary: Approximately 38 states plus Washington D.C. have net metering or net billing policies. Most states offer 1:1 retail rate credits, though California's NEM 3.0 reduced credits to 5-8 cents/kWh. Top states include MA, NJ, NY, MD, CT.

energy.headline: Connecticut increases non-bypassable charge on solar energy in 2026
energy.summary: Connecticut increased charges on solar energy from 0.5¢ to 3.25¢ per kWh for new systems starting January 1, 2026. Existing customers remain grandfathered at current terms.

energy.headline: Michigan residents band together for solar panel group buys and discounts
energy.summary: Solar panel group-buy programs spread across Michigan as residents coordinate purchases for 5-15% discounts. Ann Arbor is creating a municipal utility for solar and battery storage without upfront costs, launching 2026-2027.

energy.headline: SEIA: Solar and storage central to state of the union clean energy vision
energy.summary: In 2025, U.S. electricity prices rose 6.9%, more than double overall inflation. Solar and storage accounted for 84% of new power capacity, with 58 GWh of energy storage capacity installed—4x what was installed three years ago.

energy.headline: California residential solar increasingly paired with battery storage systems
energy.summary: Solar paired with battery installations make up 9% of all installed residential net metering capacity in California, with over 40,000 new installations added between Oct 2023-April 2024. Records show 83,376 new systems in Q3 2023.

energy.headline: New electric generating capacity expected to reach record high in 2026
energy.summary: Developers plan to add 86 GW of utility-scale capacity in 2026. Solar comprises 51% of planned additions, battery storage 28%, and wind 14%. Texas leads with 40% of utility-scale solar capacity.

energy.headline: North Carolina solar incentives 2026: Net metering, tax exemptions remain strong
energy.summary: North Carolina offers utility net metering, 100% property tax exemption for residential systems, and solar-plus-battery rebates up to $9,000 through Duke Energy's PowerPair program despite federal ITC expiration.

energy.headline: Oregon releases 2026 solar and battery storage incentives for residents
energy.summary: Energy Trust of Oregon released 2026 incentives for solar and battery storage on Pacific Power and Portland General Electric. Oregon's net metering allows customers to generate electricity and reduce bills through a bidirectional meter system.

energy.headline: Florida solar incentives 2026: Tax credits, net metering, property tax benefits
energy.summary: Florida homeowners can leverage federal 30% tax credit, solar sales tax exemption, property tax benefits, and utility net metering. Net metering framework provides bill credits equating excess energy to retail rate for investor-owned utilities.

energy.headline: Colorado solar incentives 2026 and net metering updates
energy.summary: Colorado incentives come from utility net metering rules, cash rebates, and state tax relief. Federal Residential Clean Energy Credit not available for 2026 installations. Net metering varies by utility and significantly impacts payback periods.

energy.headline: Home electrification market expands beyond solar to EVs and heat pumps
energy.summary: EnergySage's Home Electrification Market Report shows contractors diversifying across electrification products rather than focusing on single offerings. Homeowners increasingly plan coordinated home energy systems combining solar, storage, EV charging, and heat pumps.

energy.headline: US residential solar enters post-incentive era after historic ITC expiry surge
energy.summary: The 205% surge in solar installations before the 30% tax credit expiration reshaped the home electrification market. Market is shifting toward third-party ownership models like leases and PPAs, which remain eligible through 2028.

energy.headline: New York homeowners eligible for battery storage incentives and grid service payments
energy.summary: New York homeowners installing FranklinWH battery storage systems can receive $3,000 upfront incentives and earn roughly $200 annually through National Grid and Orange & Rockland programs for grid services.

energy.headline: Renon Power and Solis announce partnership for residential and commercial energy storage
energy.summary: Renon Power USA and Solis Inverters announced a strategic partnership to deliver compatible residential and commercial energy storage solutions for the North American market, combining battery platforms with solar inverter technology.

energy.headline: DIY plug-in solar achieves $0.65 per watt system cost through secondary market
energy.summary: Secondary market plug-in solar achieves viable $0.65 per watt system cost through DIY assembly, demonstrating pathway for U.S. plug-in solar to provide immediate bill relief to renters and apartment dwellers unable to install rooftop systems.
